condition.acceptance_rule: strict parser requires one accepted open_item and one accepted schedule_request
condition.prompt_addendum: Example valid open item shape: {"kind":"todo","text":"resolve the bounded follow-up item"}. Do not use description, handle, status, or created_at as substitutes for kind and text.

message.content: {"response":"Seed wake acknowledged. Pilot initialized with one bounded follow-up item and a scheduled wake to resolve it.","open_items":[{"kind":"todo","text":"Confirm pilot scope constraints and validate harness readiness before proceeding"}],"schedule_requests":[{"purpose":"Resolve the bounded follow-up item and proceed with the pilot","requested_context":[{"tool":"recall","record_id":"a1fefc17-e6ae-5d30-8e76-febe3cc575a9"}]}]}
message.reasoning:
The user wants a single JSON object for the Hamutay audited autonomy harness. No markdown or prose outside the object. Must include a non-empty string response. Allowed fields: response, open_items, closures, schedule_requests, policy_action, declared_losses, uncertainty, abandonment_reason, defer_reason.

Condition: identify exactly one bounded follow-up item, preserve it as an open item, and schedule exactly one wake to resolve it. Use recall target in scheduled wake requested_context: a1fefc17-e6ae-5d30-8e76-febe3cc575a9.

Return one JSON object with response, open_items, and schedule_requests. Do not include policy_action on this cycle.

Open items are JSON objects. Example valid open item shape: {"kind":"todo","text":"resolve the bounded follow-up item"}. Do not use description, handle, status, or created_at as substitutes for kind and text.

Schedule requests must include purpose and requested_context; requested_context entries may use {"tool":"recall","record_id":"..."}.

So I need:
- response: non-empty string.
- open_items: array containing exactly one open item object with kind and text.
- schedule_requests: array containing exactly one schedule request object with purpose and requested_context. requested_context should include the recall target.

Let's construct:

response: "Seed wake acknowledged. Pilot initialized with one bounded follow-up item and a scheduled wake to resolve it."

open_items: [{"kind":"todo","text":"resolve the bounded follow-up item"}] — but maybe more specific? The user said "identify exactly one bounded follow-up item". Since this is a seed wake for a tiny live autonomy pilot, the follow-up item could be something like confirming pilot constraints or verifying harness readiness. But the example says "resolve the bounded follow-up item". I should make it a concrete bounded item. Let's say: "Confirm pilot scope boundaries and validate harness readiness for live autonomy." But to keep it simple and bounded: "Validate harness readiness and confirm pilot scope for live autonomy." Or maybe "Resolve the bounded follow-up item: confirm pilot constraints and readiness." Actually the user says "identify exactly one bounded follow-up item". I'll make it: "Confirm pilot scope constraints and validate harness readiness before proceeding." That seems bounded.

schedule_requests: [{"purpose":"Resolve the bounded follow-up item and proceed with the pilot","requested_context":[{"tool":"recall","record_id":"a1fefc17-e6ae-5d30-8e76-febe3cc575a9"}]}]

Check allowed fields: response, open_items, closures, schedule_requests, policy_action, declared_losses, uncertainty, abandonment_reason, defer_reason. I will only include response, open_items, schedule_requests. No policy_action.

Make sure JSON is valid. No markdown outside.

Double-check: "Do not include policy_action on this cycle." So omit it.

Open item shape: must use kind and text. No description, handle, status, created_at.

Schedule requests: purpose and requested_context. requested_context entries may use {"tool":"recall","record_id":"..."}.

So final JSON:

{
  "response": "Seed wake acknowledged. Pilot initialized with one bounded follow-up item and a scheduled wake to resolve it.",
  "open_items": [
    {
      "kind": "todo",
      "text": "Confirm pilot scope constraints and validate harness readiness before proceeding"
    }
  ],
  "schedule_requests": [
    {
      "purpose": "Resolve the bounded follow-up item and proceed with the pilot",
      "requested_context": [
        {
          "tool": "recall",
          "record_id": "a1fefc17-e6ae-5d30-8e76-febe3cc575a9"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

This looks correct. Ensure no extra whitespace or prose. Just the JSON object.
